Welcome to part four of sales mastery on lease and OBS system for closing sales like crazy in this segment We're going to go into depth about a very important topic that would be closing the sale closing the sale we got the fashion plate here of GKIC and Also the sales manager extraordinary and we see and Dan Kennedy. So let's get into closing We talk a lot about that you're always closing like your entire presentation Should be a close. What does that mean? so people will Will divide this into pieces right and They will They will then get to an abrupt point That is like awkward and uncomfortable for everybody, right? and so Really a Sales presentation should be as assumptive as possible and It should be have been set up to the greatest extent possible by Marketing so that it can legitimately be Assumptive so like the ultimate of that is Where somebody is coming Knowing price or knowing price range knowing they are coming To to buy right right? so Then it falls apart only if you mess something up right right But the close should be at least seamless Not Okay now here's the call to action You know there's environments where you sort of have to do that a TV infomercial is one of them right? Right and it is jarring. It's like oh here it comes right yeah, and So they're structural elements to this there's tone. Yes the assumptive tone of Here's how this is going to work. Here's how you're going to use this here's How we're going to install it here's out from the very beginning The the other point that you raise is almost more Attitudinal that it is mechanical That you don't view it has getting to a point where now there's going to be a lot of difficulty and you have to crank up the The the power here and you have to drag or push Somebody across an imaginary line it should feel to you just as it feels to them like a natural progression From beginning to end yeah, and There's a whole energy aspect of it too and you can see this and you can feel it when somebody gets them when they think about their Presentation okay, I'm doing this now. We broke it apart into six steps But that's just to break it apart You don't actually want to think about that because you can feel when the person when it not when an amateur salesperson is Going into the clothes right they change their cadence of their voice they change their whole energy level their body tenses It just doesn't work also. I think people structure their presentations completely the wrong way they start at the beginning as Opposed okay Here's what my outcome is going to be and now I got to build everything to achieve that outcome You know I couldn't agree more I think it's painful to watch when the person on the other end isn't comfortable with closing in and They get flustered or they start talking faster start dark and slow they get staccato and it's just it is painful I think it begins with they didn't begin with the end in mind And I think they haven't come to the to the mental grips and we talked a little bit about that in the last segment the mental grips about You know if you truly believe that your product is the best solution for the for the prospect Then it's really just a natural byproduct of you asking to move forward with the relationship And you know just like I firmly believe that everybody every business owner should have magnetic marketing on their week Totally believe me on a shadow of a doubt, you know wake me up in the middle and I've gone to my head I would say that it's the important thing so every conversation should have that level of certainty that I know this will change your business life Just like it changes my business life And I think it's just part of the process and I think it comes with maturity I think it comes with professionalism I think it comes with taking the time and I think it comes with taking the respect For the for the buyer to practice it and they become comfortable with it Yeah, and during the live bonus training that we're gonna do together Nick We're gonna go over the whole mindset element of this in great depth with specific techniques that you can use Because it is it is absolutely critical. Yeah, so it's platform selling but it's It's the most profound example as I've ever seen So for a number of years we were doing seminars for dentist chiropractors Ultimately podiatrist veterinarians and I did some of them in so far the room But our main speaker fell by name of Foster. Everett. So Foster did most of the seminars and Foster We started at about 399. We wound up at about 899 is a price point, but he was basically selling a training program all audio and so the close was everybody got an order form passed out to them and Then you fill out the order forms and you know bring them back and Foster would walk them through the order forms You know on front of them now. I'm going to write my name here. You write your name It's the only funny thing you ever said you know, so I'm gonna write my name here. You write your name not my name And I'm gonna use my American Express card. You use yours not mine. It's the only funny thing you ever said which he didn't know was funny So I forget what city we were in now, but it was early in this game So I was out on the road with him We were still you know making things work and I forget where we were but there were a good 20 Maybe 30 docks in the room and they're all seated at classroom style, right? And so Foster closes finishes the clothes He now walks to the back of the room and the docs are supposed to bring their forms back there and get their bag of stuff Right and nobody moves They don't get up and go to the bathroom. They don't do nothing. They're not filling out forms. They are sitting there Like Kevin Robert used to say a tree full owls And I'm standing in the doorway, right? Because if there's like a stampede stampede, I would step in and help them to spend space and I'm watching this and I mean this is like a minute which is like Like an hour, right? It's like I'm standing back a room thinking this is not good This goes for a good three minutes and now Foster picks up a pile of the stuff He walks very slowly and sort of Royally to the front of the room all the way to the guy on the end and puts it down in front of him He now walks all the way to the back of the room He picks up another pile of stuff walks all the way back to the front of the room goes over to the second person Puts it down in front of him. He's not saying anything. Nobody's moment I'm standing in the back thinking I don't know what shit. I don't know what's gonna happen here, right? I've never seen anybody do it in my entire life He does this to every one of them and they all now are watching him their heads are turning watching him back walk to the back Back up to towards the front of the room. He did how many there were in a room 20 25 He does this takes a long time and he's not running. I mean this is 15 minutes elapsed Wow of this without a sound in the room he then comes back and Climbs up on the table and sits down and sits there That's it What's gonna happen by another three four five minutes go by and finally the first guy cracks And brings this form back right and then one after the other brings his form back Wow now I Ask him afterwards where'd you get that and of course he said what? You know I did that I didn't know I did that I must have been in a trance and off you go I don't know but hardly anybody would have right whatever you want to call it right but discipline To let this happen right most just stop all over it You know, I mean and most are afraid to leave the room I mean if I have three or four there when we've gotten to this point, I'll deliberately I haven't taken a bathroom break all day. I'm taking a bathroom break. I'm really saying talk amongst yourselves It's okay by me. I'm not worried about it. So a 1% or really So he did a lot of things right, but one of the things he did right was 80% of his sales were by appointment He created his own prospects to the magnetic marketing point Everybody else in the dealership is standing there with their donut and their cup of coffee waiting for whoever randomly Wanders in to get ups right and they're fighting over those prospects and the senior guy He maybe gets dibs so Income somebody who looks like a bum and is hopeless and he's not you go get him Dave I'm gonna wait for the next guy right it's like standing in the bank teller line and letting two people go in front of you because you want Harriet who will cash a check made out You know to a rabbit if you show up there right so So but they're all they're all they're Living off luck right he's controlling who he's spending his time with and I think that's a big differentiator Did you just mention between the 5% group and the 10% group? And then the 1% the top 1% what they're they own the script right they have the voice inflection They have the tonality they have the language down they had the front end with magnetic marketing They have the back end with the referral machine EIMT they're doing everything that we teach They've made themselves to be the experts they write the books that you'll least the special reports that type of stuff They take the business onto themselves. I also think though that they're students right so they're studying They're studying different sales environments. They're learning from everything And I think they hate losing more than they like winning or they can't stand to lose And so it drives them to be the best those are the people that you know not only said quota But you know you're you're banking on them right you're going to Arizona and buying the big timeshare Not the small timeshare when you have them on your sales force So the key is to get to them and if you get them you just take care of them You don't mess with their quotas you don't mess with their commissions You don't do all the stuff that sometimes organizations do you really really really take care of that 1% You just have something you've found things not many not many shit that many during the four hours But a couple no again what you just mentioned I think is really really powerful Magnetic marketing is how you generate the leads right generate quality prospects What we're doing right now in teaching is how to close those how to turn those leads into prospects customers clients patients The final piece not the final piece But a big piece of this puzzle is the ultimate referral machine Which is okay, so now you got this person and now you can turn them into a referral so those I would say those are Gkic's three core products We didn't have this piece right here right that we're doing today. We were missing the middle piece and so fantastic I think boxing matches are seldom one-on-one punch. They're one on combinations, right? And I think magnetic marketing is used with the sales mastery used with the ultimate referral machine is the phenomenal combination Yeah, you know to to get the knockout one of my favorite things then I learned from you is takeaway selling To me this is one of them the most powerful strategies that that there is and not a lot of people use it Talk about what takeaway selling is and how someone can actually use it in their business because it's crazy powerful when people have to qualify and They have they feel like they have to sell you on Accepting them a lot of your Your own selling now becomes very pro forma It becomes very relaxed Price negotiation price problems tend to go away The kind of prospect you attract Tends to be a higher Level of prospect, you know that so all of the very expensive kind of many in buildings in New York City 900 square foot apartments being sold for $15 million You can't just walk in there and buy it if you got $15 million and everybody knows it you have to get through the board of other residents and The rep the people who are managing the building and so forth and you have to like qualify In order to be allowed to buy that and allowed to move into that building well That process alone See it changes The real estate agent now representing this doesn't really have a price conversation right they have a I Need to help you right right get accepted. Let's work together to build your kids right? Yeah? Yeah? Yeah, I'm not sure I can get you in them, right? Right and The more of that that occurs the more the person wants to be in say so now It doesn't have granted countertops Yeah That window is this much smaller that see you don't have that when you're selling a $200,000 house To John and Mary you got all that crap right the real estate agent has to deal with it all Right the window faces in the wrong direction the bathrooms too small the this stuff's out the window, right? No, it's My body right and can you get me in? So when you create that and people greatly underestimate How it can be? Created and the kinds of businesses it can be created in when you create that it's not that you negate selling You just get to sell at a much higher level With a lot more price of last destiny And a lot less stress for everybody Yeah, that's exactly I keep going back to when I was selling over the telephone one on one literally one call $20,000 closes and that's exactly the whole thing is they were trying to sell themselves on me I structured it like I was a got the raker and people were coming to pitch me their ideas now They had to give me money, but it would just change the whole dynamic and it makes selling so easy the high-end place here Right there's one in Virginia, too, but the one here where we board the million dollar dog In order to be allowed the open The play time during the day with the other dogs the dog has to take an assessment Sure, you know a psychological test to see whether or not it's okay to put the dog in there so the conversation about How much does it cost to put the dog here for a day? Yeah, I mean it was here's the other six things You gotta do awesome, Mary So well great stuff great great stuff Okay, so sometimes though we don't have a lot of folks don't have a one-step sales process like if I'm so going out selling a jet I'm probably not making a one-call close on that So I wouldn't rule it out, but you're right more often than not so what happens when you have to make to sell In a multiple-step sales process Well, I think the first thing you do you do is you don't make the mistake that are fronted earlier and not having all the Decision makers in the room when you're putting that together. So that's step number one So anybody that the final decision makers need to be in the room So once you have that it's you're gonna have to I use the covered wagon, you know You need to you circle them make sure that nobody else could penetrate that circle and that's through marketing That's through shock in all boxes. That's you're gonna do some social, you know Media searches to make sure that you have the understanding of what their hot buttons are in realizing at different levels of the organization It's pain and pleasure, right? So some people are gonna not make decisions just because they're afraid or they don't want to lose their job or be a fear of being wrong So you need to take that off the table for them and there's others that you know If they're the CEO he wants to drive the top line or he wants to improve the bottom line And so you need to talk to that language. You need to understand all the players who are your angels within the organization What's their motivation you define a rabbi somebody to kind of carry you through the organization? But you really don't make the mistake that our friend did in the second segment of not having the all the decision makers in the room Every time you're doing a presentation. Yeah, and look you can if you know that that is the case Okay, which you should always give somebody an opportunity to circumvent it if they wish because there are leapers at Every dollar amount, right? I mean when I Switch to flying private. So you want to be in that game When you can't be with him you want to be with him, right? I have a client coming Thursday. It's exactly this so he knows me knows of me Really probably ready to hire me. He thinks at least he knows what he wants So the start of that conversation is well, I don't want to answer that because that number You know out of context is going to be to him a holy shit number, right? And then it's going to be all my four guys on my other P okay, and I got it so now I'm going to be into So I'd rather sell the diagnostic sure right which is a twenty thousand dollar decision and Here's how we do this around here, right? And so you buy the diagnostic, okay? I had a client weight loss Changed his entire practice because he's selling very expensive $15,000 give or take weight loss program with lead generation advertising Driving to an appointment right and his closing rate Terrible and he was a good salesman, so he was doing pretty much everything right with the sale selling itself and closing 10% of these people Right, I said I got to fix this for you. I said one Let's rule out some people in the advertising itself who really are not going to spend $15,000 to lose weight Let's drive them away to start with right Secondly, let's make them pay for the appointment Right, we want to make a $15,000 sale here. Um, I think that also If you are 35 selling to that market you will make assumptions based on your own behavior That are not at all universally true All right, so one of the biggest problems now in business to business with sales forces I have a client right now where we're about to clean house is The sales people insist on just emailing the proposals And everybody's confused about why They're not closing And even to a 30 year old buyer at the other end That's not going to close a sale. The the sales are going to remain the same We talked about that. There was an article in the wall street journal about a woman that owned a business that had all millennium Sellers, exactly and she had to stop them from sending emails and make phone calls and her sales went up 25% the first month and You know dramatically since then so, you know, there's a bias to think well All everybody wants to do is send emails out and you could get an auto responder today Well, one of the one of the the easiest things not I shouldn't say easiest But one of the best things if someone's watches wants to improve their business Is to is to have a salesperson. Yes. Yeah, absolutely No question. I'm going to give you these scripts and you've got to memorize these scripts There's going to be that pushback But if they listen to the whole section on why you need to script your presentation They're going to get it It's going to make a lot of sense to them and no one's going to say well I don't want to be a professional and let's make as much money as I can make so take advantage of this Get this right now.
